title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Bohemish for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for June.

🦖 Gluten Free Sours from Xul
• Space Man From Pluto Sour with Raspberries
• Welcome to Bedrock Sour with Tangerine, Coconut, Vanilla & Marshmallows
• PB&J Mixtape returns!

Lager time with Fair State

• Side Pull Czech Pale Lager - collab with Bierstadt Lagerhaus!

• Foamers American Lager

👊 DC fav Lost Generation
• The Vanity of It All Italian Pilsner
• Back to Oblivion Hazy IPA with Nelson & Citra

Norfolk's own Afterglow

• Regular Beer American Lager

• That's What She Saaz Dark Czech Lager

• Backstage Burton Foeder Aged Burton Ale

The Veil dropping the hops
• New Mirage 3 Hazy IPA TDH with Mosaic, Nelson, Simcoe & Citra
• All Green Everything DDH TIPA with Citra, Mosaic & Motueka - collab with Other Half

🛥️ A bangin variant from Icarus
• DDH Mosaic Yacht Juice DIPA with Citra, Mosaic & Columbus, DDH with Mosaic Lupulin

Here for Vasen
• Third Drop Pils Czech Pilsner
• Mountain Pass IPA with Riwaka & Rakau

Evil Twin doing the most
• Our Most Popular Summer Witbier
• I Always Look Forward to the Summer of Hedonism DDH IPA with Citra & Motueka

Local Hops

• Coastal Fabricated Disbelief Hazy DIPA with Citra, Amarillo & Simcoe

• DC Brau Baby Wings Session West Coast IPA

Fun for the whole fam
• Potter's Passionfruit Mosaic Cider
• Two Roads Two Juicy N/A Hazy IPA
• Sixpoint Variety Pack

💋 Something for everyone from Commonwealth
• Bisou Bisou French Pilsner with Barbe Rouge & Saphir
• Josephine Bavarian Hefeweizen
• Too Cool For British Rule Hazy IPA with Southern Sublime & Mosaic
• Mean Girls Gose with Mango, Blackberry, Vanilla & Honey

 Go Big or Go Home with Adroit Theory

• Backmasking Russian Imperial Stout

• Atomic Age Russian Imperial Stout with Coffee

• All Shall Perish Hazy DIPA with Mosaic, Citra & Nelson

• Incursion Hazy Triple IPA with Citra, Mosaic & Nelson

Sojourn / Good Word In Good Company West Coast IPA

Final Gravity Astro Hazy IPA

New wines in the door!

Born Rambla Rose: What started as a passion project for a group of women in Barcelona has turned into one serious, but crushworthy bottle of Rose. I'm declaring Rambla Rose the top bottle to snatch for parties, picnics or pool sipping. Ballet pink with notes of peach, strawberry, citrus and a dry, mineral-tinged finish. Organic.

Semeli Estate Feast White: Moschofilero is a pink-skinned grape variety native to Greece known for its aromatic intensity. Despite the pink hue of the grape, Feast White is indeed a white wine. Fresh cut flowers fill your glass with notes of citrus blossom, jasmine, mandarin orange and the best squirt of acidity.

Flores Blanc de Blancs Sparkling: Grown in the rugged Extremadura region on the western edge of the Iberian Peninsula, this Traditional Method sparkler is lively and refreshing. Check out Aidan’s writeup, below, for all the details.

The Diver Brut Rose: The Diver Brut Rose comes to us from a mother-daughter duo, Madigan and Abigail Ahn, who live by the philosophy that great wine should be delicious, approachable, and made for any occasion, not just special ones. As a fan of Tuesday night bubbly, I whole-heartedly agree! Aromas of red berries and honeysuckle are followed by notes of strawberry shortcake, cherry, and kiwi, all wrapped in a soft, Prosecco-like texture from the Charmat method.

 

Born Rambla Rose: What started as a passion project for a group of women in Barcelona has turned into one serious, but crushworthy bottle of Rose. I'm declaring Rambla Rose the top bottle to snatch for parties, picnics or pool sipping. Ballet pink with notes of peach, strawberry, citrus and a dry, mineral-tinged finish. Organic.

Semeli Estate Feast White: Moschofilero is a pink-skinned grape variety native to Greece known for its aromatic intensity. Despite the pink hue of the grape, Feast White is indeed a white wine. Fresh cut flowers fill your glass with notes of citrus blossom, jasmine, mandarin orange and the best squirt of acidity.

Flores Blanc de Blancs Sparkling: Grown in the rugged Extremadura region on the western edge of the Iberian Peninsula, this Traditional Method sparkler is lively and refreshing. Check out Aidan’s writeup, below, for all the details.

The Diver Brut Rose: The Diver Brut Rose comes to us from a mother-daughter duo, Madigan and Abigail Ahn, who live by the philosophy that great wine should be delicious, approachable, and made for any occasion, not just special ones. As a fan of Tuesday night bubbly, I whole-heartedly agree! Aromas of red berries and honeysuckle are followed by notes of strawberry shortcake, cherry, and kiwi, all wrapped in a soft, Prosecco-like texture from the Charmat method.

I have a soft spot for Spanish wine. It consistently delivers everything I want: freshness, character, and the sneaking suspicion that I somehow got away with paying less than I should have. New to the shop this week (and only $14.99 on the shelf), Flores Blanc de Blancs by Carolina Mata is yet another score from Spain.

Made using the traditional method, this sparkling wine blends Macabeo, Parellada, and a small amount of Alarije, a grape local to Extremadura. Think white flowers, lemon peel, green apple, and pear, all carried along by bright acidity and a stream of fine bubbles. It does spend 9 months on the lees, so if you're hoping for a sprinkle of briochiness you won't be disappointed.

Carolina Mata, whose love of flowers inspired both the label and the wine itself, definitely understood the assignment. Not every sparkling wine needs to be reserved for weddings, promotions, or big milestones (coming from someone who has had the same bottle of Champagne for over a year and a half trying to decide on the right occasion to open it....). Sometimes you just want bubbles because it’s Tuesday, or you successfully answered an email you've been avoiding for three days, or your partner is out of town and you want some bubbly to pair with your Chipotle while you veg out on the couch and watch TV. This wine feels equally at home next to a platter of jamón and Manchego as it does alongside a bag of potato chips standing over the kitchen counter. That's what I appreciate the most about Spanish wines: no frills, no fuss, perfect for a fiesta or a siesta.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Aidan.

June Tasting Events coming your way!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
